beats.narration_text: This is Kore for Humanitarians AI.

props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · Build This — Ogilvy Voice (Kore)

beats.narration_text: Right now there's a short window where you can hire an engineer good enough for FAANG — before FAANG hires them. This is Kore, in for Humanitarians AI. Let me show you the window.
beats.new_visual_element: Opening title card — 'Build This' in large serif, terracotta period. Subtitle: 'The FAANG window.' Newsprint ground. Cream + charcoal.

beats.narration_text: Here is why Build This — Ogilvy Voice (Kore) belongs in your working knowledge. The systems, policies, and tools shaped by it affect decisions at your level — whether or not you understand the mechanism. Knowing the underlying logic gives you the frame; missing it means reasoning from output with no access to the inputs.

I teach and advise these graduates. I know which ones are the real thing — I've watched them work. Most are deep in a FAANG job search right now, and that search takes months. So for those months, they're free to build. That's the window.
beats.new_visual_element: Isotype dot grid — rows of engineer silhouettes, subset highlighted in crimson: 'the ones Bear knows.' Timeline bar below: FAANG search = months. Label chips: 'Available now' (dusty navy) vs 'Hired' (ink).
beats.spark_line: Good enough for FAANG. Free while they wait.

beats.narration_text: This isn't a job board. I match the right person to the work — web developers for web, AI specialists for AI. Before anyone writes code, we write the spec together, so you see exactly what you're paying for. And when they hit a wall, I'm on the call.
beats.new_visual_element: Three-column document card drawn on newsprint: MATCH (web→web / AI→AI) → SPEC (written before code, hand-drawn underline animates) → SUPPORT (Bear on call, telephone icon draw-on). Terracotta annotation arrows between columns.
beats.spark_line: Spec before code. Bear on the call.

beats.narration_text: Thirty-five dollars an hour. A FAANG engineer costs six figures and takes months to land — you get the same caliber, today, and you pay only for the hours you use. Ten one week, more the next, none if you like. No retainer. And if your grad lands the job they were chasing, I bring the next one up to speed. You don't lose a day.
beats.new_visual_element: Two-column bar comparison. Left: '$35/hr' bar (dusty navy, short). Right: '$200+/hr · months to hire' (terracotta, tall). Below bars: hand-off guarantee line — grad silhouette replaces grad silhouette with continuity arrow. Label: 'You don't lose a day.'

beats.narration_text: They get real work and income while they search. You get the build at today's rate — before the market catches up and the window closes.
beats.new_visual_element: GRAPHIC: two-column win-win layout — Grad (teal): real income + portfolio work. Founder (terracotta): build ships + today's rate. Built via Manim.

beats.narration_text: Bring me your idea. I'll match you to the person who can build it. bearbrown.co — that's where it starts. Kore, in for Humanitarians AI. From Humanitarians AI.
beats.new_visual_element: CTA card — newsprint ground. Large serif: 'bearbrown.co'. Below: '#BuildThis' (terracotta hairline). Handle '@HumanitariansAI' bottom right. 'Kore, in for Humanitarians AI.' sign-off beneath.
